commands.review_summary:
The branch defaults lean local-model runs to structured Tool Search when `tools.toolSearch` is unset, threads that runtime config through embedded-runner tool construction and cataloging, and updates focused tests plus local-model docs.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +85, Docs +2. Total +122 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main leaves `tools.toolSearch` disabled when unset while lean mode only filters the direct heavy tools. I did not establish a live local-model failure path on current main in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed to accept or reject the compatibility impact of changing lean-mode defaults; there is no narrow automated repair indicated.

target.title: fix(agents): compact lean local tool catalogs
target.body:
## Summary

- default `agents.*.experimental.localModelLean: true` runs to structured Tool Search controls when the operator has not configured `tools.toolSearch`
- keep explicit operator Tool Search config untouched, including `tools.toolSearch: false`
- thread the lean runtime config through embedded attempt tool construction, catalog compaction, and client-tool cataloging so large plugin/MCP/client catalogs stay behind `tool_search`, `tool_describe`, and `tool_call`
- document the expanded lean-mode behavior in local model and Ollama guidance

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed on final head `957c5d468cf`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md` - passed on final head `957c5d468cf`.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - passed on final head `957c5d468cf`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 2 files / 65 tests passed on final head `957c5d468cf`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings; `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_261e7f93ddd3`, slug `golden-hermit`, run `run_5ccda3e85dc8`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `docs`, base `425a4ab2f2b`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: lean local-model runs now keep large tool catalogs behind structured Tool Search controls by default instead of exposing every callable schema directly to weak/local models.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a linked worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.
Evidence after fix: refreshed head `957c5d468cf`; `local-model-lean` coverage proves the default is applied only for lean runs and preserves explicit operator config; embedded attempt coverage proves lean runs pass Tool Search controls into tool construction; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed core, coreTests, and docs lanes.
Observed result after fix: a lean run with no `tools.toolSearch` config gets `{ enabled: true, mode: "tools", searchDefaultLimit: 5, maxSearchLimit: 10 }`; a lean run with explicit `tools.toolSearch: false` remains opted out.
What was not tested: live Ollama/local-model runtime was not run for this PR; this is the prompt/tool-surface mitigation in the broader small-model stability set, paired with the dense-stream mitigation in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87818.
